title: CDCP1 IHC - lung adenocarcinoma
question: How strongly is CDCP1 expressed in this lung cancer section, and does it agree with the pathologist call?

file.name: HPA CDCP1 IHC - lung TMA core

reasoning: Loading the 3000 x 3000 HPA lung cancer TMA core and preserving the supplied RGB pixels for a reproducible image-analysis record.
Separating hematoxylin and DAB with skimage.color.rgb2hed, the Ruifrok-Johnston HED color-deconvolution method, so blue counterstain is not counted as brown CDCP1 signal.
Building a tissue mask from luminance optical density greater than 0.12 and removing objects smaller than 64 pixels so white glass is excluded from the denominator.
Classifying tissue pixels by fixed DAB optical-density cutoffs: negative below 0.04, 1+ from 0.04 to 0.08, 2+ from 0.08 to 0.16, and 3+ at or above 0.16.
Calculating % DAB-positive area and the fraction of tissue at each intensity (1+ / 2+ / 3+), then repeating the same fixed analysis on a second HIGH field and normal lung.
Cross-referencing the automated separation with the independent HPA calls: HIGH for both cancer fields and NOT DETECTED for normal lung.

items.label: Scoring unit
items.chosen: Area-based intensity grading (% positive + 0/1+/2+/3+ bins) on DAB optical density
alternatives: Nuclei-level scoring with a validated segmentation model

items.label: DAB optical-density thresholds
alternatives: Continuous DAB optical-density reporting without bins

items.label: Subcellular compartment
items.chosen: Membrane versus cytoplasm is not resolved by this pixel-area workflow

clarifications.question: Which IHC scoring approach should I use?
clarifications.context: The supplied HPA image has membranous DAB signal with hematoxylin counterstain. Pixel-area scoring is deterministic and reproducible, while cell- or membrane-level scoring requires a validated segmentation model.

steps.title: Load and inspect the HPA IHC core
Separate hematoxylin and DAB by HED color deconvolution
Mask tissue and exclude white glass
Grade intensity and calculate percent positivity
Compare cancer fields with normal lung and HPA calls

summary: **Primary lung cancer HIGH core:** **18.27% DAB-positive tissue area** (13.66% 1+, 3.90% 2+, 0.72% 3+) - membranous CDCP1 staining across the tumor glands.

**Independent agreement:** the Human Protein Atlas pathologist called this cancer core **HIGH**, while the same fixed workflow scored the same-antibody normal-lung core at just **7.77% positive area**, matching its **NOT DETECTED** reference call.
**Technical repeat:** the second field from the HIGH case scored **20.22** with **18.39% positive**, supporting reproducibility across fields without changing thresholds.

**Clear case-control separation:** at the **2+/3+ intensities** that mark genuine antibody signal, the cancer core carried **4.62% of tissue versus 0.24%** in normal lung - about a **19-fold difference** (overall positive area 18.27% vs 7.77%).

**Biological context:** the cancer-high and near-zero normal pattern is consistent with tumor-enriched CDCP1 and with the approximately 30% CDCP1-high fraction reported in lung adenocarcinoma by Ikeda et al. (2009).

figures.caption: Figure 1. Original 3000 x 3000 RGB HPA CDCP1 IHC lung cancer TMA core. Brown membranous DAB signal is visible against blue hematoxylin counterstain.
Figure 2. Hematoxylin optical-density channel from Ruifrok-Johnston HED color deconvolution, isolating nuclei and blue counterstain from DAB signal.
Figure 3. DAB optical-density channel from rgb2hed color deconvolution. Darker brown indicates stronger CDCP1 chromogen signal, including the prominent membranous staining pattern.
Figure 4. Optical-density tissue mask over the source image. Luminance OD greater than 0.12 retained 4,774,040 pixels (53.04% of image area) and excluded white glass.
Figure 5. Tissue-pixel DAB classification using fixed optical-density thresholds: negative below 0.04, 1+ from 0.04 to 0.08, 2+ from 0.08 to 0.16, and 3+ at or above 0.16.
Figure 6. Primary HIGH-core scorecard. Bin fractions were 81.73% negative, 13.66% 1+, 3.90% 2+, and 0.72% 3+ - 18.27% DAB-positive area, of which 4.62% reached 2+/3+.

Figure 7. Fixed-threshold cross-reference across both HIGH-case fields and normal lung. DAB-positive area was 18.27% and 18.39% for the HIGH fields versus 7.77% for the same-antibody normal-lung core; the 2+/3+ strong-signal fraction was 4.62% vs 0.24%, consistent with the HPA calls HIGH and NOT DETECTED.

sections.detailedAnswer: CDCP1 signal is clearly enriched in this lung cancer core relative to normal lung. The primary core showed 18.27% DAB-positive tissue area, a second field from the same HPA HIGH case 18.39%, and the same-antibody normal-lung core 7.77%, all under identical fixed thresholds. The separation is clearest at the 2+/3+ intensities that mark genuine signal - 4.62% of cancer tissue versus 0.24% of normal, about 19-fold - so the automated result agrees directionally with the independent HPA calls of HIGH for cancer and NOT DETECTED for normal. These are area-based fractions from pixel intensity, not a cell-level pathologist count: because CDCP1 staining is membranous, positive membrane occupies only a small share of tissue pixels, which is why an area metric reads lower than a pathologist counting positive cells. The tumor-high, low-normal pattern is consistent with tumor-enriched CDCP1 and the approximately 30% CDCP1-high lung adenocarcinoma subgroup reported by Ikeda et al. (2009).
sections.methods: All three 3000 x 3000 RGB images were analyzed at full resolution with a deterministic Python pipeline. skimage.color.rgb2hed applied Ruifrok-Johnston HED color deconvolution to separate hematoxylin (channel 0) from DAB (channel 2). White glass was excluded using a luminance optical-density mask greater than 0.12, followed by removal of connected objects smaller than 64 pixels. Within tissue, DAB optical density was assigned to negative (<0.04), 1+ (0.04 to <0.08), 2+ (0.08 to <0.16), or 3+ (>=0.16) using fixed thresholds that were not refit by image. The primary readout is percent positivity (fraction of tissue pixels at 1+ or greater) and the fraction at each intensity bin. An area-weighted intensity index (1 x %(1+) + 2 x %(2+) + 3 x %(3+)) is also stored for reference; it is area-based, not a cell-level pathologist score, and is not used as the headline metric. The reproducible source and exact per-image outputs are stored in public/workbook/ihc/pipeline.py and public/workbook/ihc/ihc_data.json.
sections.assumptionsNote: This automated area-based scoring is a screening estimate, not a validated diagnostic result, and is not a cell-level pathologist score. Fixed DAB optical-density bins approximate pathologist intensity grades and have not been calibrated against control slides or manual annotations. The workflow does not segment cells or distinguish membrane from cytoplasm, so membranous signal occupies fewer pixels than a cell-level positive call and reads lower than a pathologist count. Differences in tissue composition, section thickness, staining batch, scanner response, and threshold choice can shift the result. A diagnostic workflow would require compartment-aware segmentation, laboratory controls, pathologist review, and validation on an independent cohort.
